summary: DHH returns from a two-week blogging hiatus with a New Year's resolution to write regularly. He notes he has plenty of topics queued up and attempts to use a public commitment as motivation to force himself to write. The post is self-awarely humorous, ending with an update three days later admitting the public commitment strategy failed.
key_points: DHH resolves to write on a regular basis as his single New Year's resolution
He keeps a bedside list of eight potential post ideas, showing no shortage of opinions
He experiments with public accountability as a writing motivator
The self-deprecating update reveals the strategy immediately failed
money_quotes: My single resolution for the new year is to write on a regular basis.
How clever of me to use public commitments to force writing!
key_insight: Public commitments alone are not enough to sustain a writing habit, as DHH's immediate failure humorously demonstrates.
